name: Adrenalectomy (laparoscopic)
sources: Model-generated (textbook-level); verify against an operative atlas / SAGES / urologic surgery text. Left lateral-transperitoneal anatomy reviewed by the user (medical illustrator).
review_note: Side-dependent venous drainage is the top error: LEFT central vein drains into the LEFT RENAL VEIN; RIGHT central vein drains directly into the IVC. Approach-dependent exposure: lateral transperitoneal reflects the spleen and pancreatic tail medially; posterior retroperitoneal does NOT (direct posterior access).

intake.why: Determines composition and how much can be shown clearly
Side determines venous drainage: left -> left renal vein, right -> IVC
Determines exposure: lateral transperitoneal reflects spleen/pancreas (left) or liver (right); posterior retroperitoneal accesses the gland directly from behind without mobilizing them
One figure cannot show everything; focus drives which step dominates

figure_scopes.overview: Adrenal gland in situ — superomedial to the kidney upper pole, with the central vein to its drainage (left renal vein on the left, IVC on the right) and spleen/pancreas/aorta relations
figure_scopes.storyboard: Lateral transperitoneal (left): positioning/ports -> splenic flexure + spleen/pancreatic tail medial reflection -> open Gerota and expose the gland -> control the central vein at its drainage -> periadrenal dissection -> retrieval
figure_scopes.closeup: Central vein draining into its vessel (left renal vein / IVC)

laterality: Adrenal sits superomedial to the upper pole of the kidney within Gerota's fascia. LEFT central vein drains into the left renal vein; RIGHT central vein drains directly into the IVC. In the lateral transperitoneal approach the spleen and pancreatic tail (left) or liver (right) are reflected to expose the gland; the posterior retroperitoneal approach does not mobilize them.
must_have: Adrenal gland shown as a distinct golden, lobulated body superomedial to the upper pole of the kidney, separate from the renal parenchyma.
Adrenal central vein and the vessel it drains into (left renal vein on the left; IVC on the right).
Kidney upper pole and Gerota's fascia.
For the lateral transperitoneal left approach: spleen, pancreatic tail, splenic flexure, and splenorenal/splenophrenic attachments.
spatial_rules: LEFT adrenal central vein drains into the left renal vein; RIGHT adrenal central vein drains directly into the IVC. Draw the destination for the chosen side.
The adrenal gland lies superomedial to the upper pole of the kidney and is a discrete gland, not fused with the renal parenchyma.
Lateral transperitoneal (left): spleen and pancreatic tail are anterior and are reflected medially to expose the retroperitoneum; the posterior retroperitoneal approach does not mobilize them.
Stay in the periadrenal (capsular) plane; suprarenal arteries are small and multiple (from inferior phrenic artery, aorta, and renal artery).

forbidden_errors.error: Adrenal central vein drawn to the wrong destination for the chosen side (left into the IVC, or right into the renal vein).
Wrong side depicted relative to the request.
Renal vein or renal artery clipped/divided as if it were the adrenal central vein.
Spleen or pancreatic tail injured, or exposure shown without reflecting them in the lateral transperitoneal approach.
Adrenal gland not identifiable as a discrete gland (fused into the kidney upper pole).
Spleen/pancreas reflection drawn for a posterior retroperitoneal approach, which does not mobilize them.

safety_concept: Identify and control the adrenal central vein at its drainage (left renal vein on the left, IVC on the right); in the lateral transperitoneal approach reflect the spleen and pancreatic tail without injury; stay in the periadrenal plane and protect the renal vessels and kidney.
phases: Patient in lateral decubitus (operative side up) and trocar placement.
Mobilize the splenic flexure; divide the splenorenal and splenophrenic attachments.
Reflect the spleen and pancreatic tail medially to expose the retroperitoneum and the superomedial upper renal pole.
Open Gerota's fascia and identify the discrete golden adrenal gland.
Identify and control the adrenal central vein at the left renal vein.
Periadrenal dissection controlling small arteries, then bag and extract the gland.
verify: Adrenal is a discrete golden gland superomedial to the kidney upper pole, separate from renal parenchyma.
Central vein drains into the correct vessel for the side (left renal vein on the left; IVC on the right).
For lateral transperitoneal left: spleen and pancreatic tail reflected medially and intact; Gerota opened.
Renal vein, renal artery, and kidney preserved and not mistaken for the adrenal vein.
